---
title: "Arnold Salvacion"
about:
    template: trestles
    links:
        - icon: github
          text: Github
          url: https://github.com/arsalvacion
        - icon: linkedin
          text: LinkedIn
          url: https://www.linkedin.com/in/arnold-salvacion-98166949/
        - text: "{{< ai clarivate >}} Web of Science"
          url: https://www.webofscience.com/wos/author/record/1040244
        - text: "{{< ai scopus >}} Scopus"
          url: https://www.scopus.com/authid/detail.uri?authorId=56090830200
        - text: "{{< ai orcid >}} 0000-0001-8868-2226"
          url: https://orcid.org/0000-0001-8868-2226
        - text: "{{< ai researchgate >}} ResearchGate"
          url: https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Arnold-Salvacion
        - text: "{{< ai google-scholar >}} Google Scholar"
          url: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=OH852c8AAAAJ&hl=en
          
image: prof2.jpg
comments: false
---

## About Me

I am a Research Fellow at the [Centre for Crop and Disease Management](https://www.ccdm.com.au/people/arnold-salvacion/), Curtin University, where I lead analytics development for Western Australian grain farmers through the Curtin for Agribusiness Profitability (C4AP) program. With over 15 years of research experience, my work spans environmental science, applied climate science, human ecology, and geospatial analysis, with a particular focus on addressing agricultural resilience and natural hazard management in Southeast Asia and Australia.

In 2021, I was recognized as an [Outstanding Young Scientist](https://recognition.nast.ph/index.php/nast-awards/outstanding-young-scientist) by the National Academy of Science and Technology (Philippines) for my contributions to advancing human ecological research. My research portfolio includes significant work in flood hazard mapping, drought risk assessment, and landslide susceptibility modeling—developing practical, cost-effective solutions for disaster risk reduction in vulnerable communities. Prior to joining Curtin University, I served as Associate Professor at the University of the Philippines Los Baños, where I established foundational research in spatially integrated human ecology.

I am the founding Editor-in-Chief of the [Journal of Human Ecology and Sustainability](https://jhes.uplb.edu.ph/) and serve on the editorial boards of [PeerJ](https://peerj.com/arsalvacion/), [Spatial Information Research](https://link.springer.com/journal/41324/editors), and [SN Social Sciences](https://link.springer.com/journal/43545). My work bridges the gap between cutting-edge geospatial science and real-world applications in agriculture, natural resource management, and climate adaptation.

## Awards and Recognition

-   UP Scientist III (2023-2025) - University of the Philippines Systems
-   Outstanding Young Scientist (2021) - National Academy of Science and Technology
-   UP Scientist I (2020-2022) - University of the Philippines Systems
-   OneUP Professorial Chair Grant for Research and Public Service (Environmental Science) (2022-2024) - University of the Philippines Systems
-   Enhanced Creative Work and Research Grant (ECWRG) (2021-2022) - University of the Philippines Systems
-   OneUP Faculty Grant for Teaching and Research (Environmental Science) (2019-2021) - University of the Philippines Systems
-   UP International Publications Awards (2015-2023) - University of the Philippines Systems
